reference_answer: Authentication is done using tokens like personal access tokens or GitHub App tokens.
Using authenticated requests and avoiding excessive requests helps prevent rate limits.
Too many concurrent requests can trigger secondary rate limits.
A REST request includes method, path, headers, authentication, and parameters.
The list organization repositories endpoint returns repositories for a given organization.
Unauthenticated requests have lower rate limits and less access.
Webhooks are recommended instead of polling to avoid repeated requests.
Repository contents endpoints return file or directory contents.
Repository statistics endpoints provide data about repository activity.
The questions endpoint returns questions with filters like score and date.
GitHub supports personal tokens, app tokens, and GITHUB_TOKEN.
Polling should be avoided to reduce unnecessary API calls.
The answers endpoint returns undeleted answers.
Some fields require higher permissions to be visible.
Query parameters provide additional information for requests.
